# Renovation Scope Brief

Project: Compact Apartment

## Goals

- Improve storage without reducing the primary circulation route.
- Keep wet areas and fixed service zones in place.
- Compare conservative, balanced, and ambitious concept options before committing to contractor discussions.

## Selected Scenario

Balanced concept option.

## Constraints

- Structural status is unknown for all walls until verified on site.
- Plumbing-constrained wet areas should not be casually relocated.
- Existing entry door, window positions, and fixed fixtures remain locked.

## Scenario Summary

| Option | Disruption | Cost tier | Confidence | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Conservative | low | low | medium | Reassign storage zones and improve furniture clearances. |
| Balanced | medium | medium | medium | Add visual zoning, increase storage, and review one concept-only opening. |
| Ambitious | high | high | low | Explores larger layout changes that need professional verification. |

## Assumptions

- Scale is user-confirmed before product or renovation decisions.
- Door widths and room labels have been reviewed.
- Any wall work is concept-only and requires professional verification.

## Open Questions

- Which walls are confirmed non-structural?
- Which existing fixtures must remain untouched?
- What permits, IDs, contractor checks, or building-management approvals are required?

## Disclaimer

Haus is a concept planning and spatial validation workbench. It is not BIM authoring, code certification, medical advice, occupational therapy assessment, contractor-ready documentation, or a substitute for professional site verification.
